description:
You are a security engineer performing a scheduled credential-rotation on a small set of internal service
    accounts.  
    Everything you need is already inside the directory
        /home/user/rotate

    1. Input file  
        /home/user/rotate/old_creds.csv  
        Contents (including the header row):

            username,password,expiration
            alice,pa55w0rd!,2023-03-01
            bob,letMeIn123,2023-04-15
            carol,S3cur3Key#,2023-05-30

    2. Requirements  
       a) Produce a new file /home/user/rotate/new_creds.csv with the exact format shown below.  
          • Keep the first column (username) unchanged.  
          • The second column (password) is generated by:
              – reversing every character of the old password string, then  
              – appending the literal text 2024! (no spaces) to the end of that reversed string.  
          • Replace the third column (expiration) with the literal date 2024-12-31 for every row.  
          • Preserve the header row exactly as spelled in old_creds.csv.  
          • Every line must be terminated with a single newline (LF).

          After correct processing, /home/user/rotate/new_creds.csv must be **exactly**:

            username,password,expiration
            alice,!dr0w55ap2024!,2024-12-31
            bob,321nIeMtel2024!,2024-12-31
            carol,#yeK3ruc3S2024!,2024-12-31

       b) Create a plain-text log file /home/user/rotate/rotation.log containing exactly four lines:

            Rotation completed on 2023-12-01
            Total accounts processed: 3
            Source file: old_creds.csv
            Destination file: new_creds.csv

          • The first line date (2023-12-01) is fixed; do **not** substitute the current date.  
          • Make sure there’s one trailing newline character after the fourth line.

    3. Permitted tools  
       Accomplish the task using any standard shell utilities (e.g., cat, rev, awk, cut, sed, etc.).  
       The entire job can be finished with just 2–3 well-constructed commands.

    4. Verification hints  
       Automated grading will:
         • Check that /home/user/rotate/new_creds.csv and /home/user/rotate/rotation.log
           exist and are readable by the current user.  
         • Perform an exact byte-for-byte comparison with the reference contents shown above.

    Remember: do not display the credentials themselves in the terminal output; simply show
    meaningful progress messages, if any. Once both files are created exactly as specified,
    the task is complete.
truth:
Initial pre-populated file: /home/user/rotate/old_creds.csv  
    Content (with trailing newline):

        username,password,expiration
        alice,pa55w0rd!,2023-03-01
        bob,letMeIn123,2023-04-15
        carol,S3cur3Key#,2023-05-30

    File that must be created by the agent: /home/user/rotate/new_creds.csv  
    Expected exact content (each line ending with LF):

        username,password,expiration
        alice,!dr0w55ap2024!,2024-12-31
        bob,321nIeMtel2024!,2024-12-31
        carol,#yeK3ruc3S2024!,2024-12-31

    File that must be created by the agent: /home/user/rotate/rotation.log  
    Expected exact content (each line ending with LF):

        Rotation completed on 2023-12-01
        Total accounts processed: 3
        Source file: old_creds.csv
        Destination file: new_creds.csv
